autofix_suggestions.rationale: Hoare-style safety obligations need explicit guards/effects so failed weakest preconditions point to concrete runbook edits.
autofix_suggestions.title: Add missing semantic preconditions/effects

findings.excerpt: > If you can isolate the impacted tenants, attempt to take targeted action instead of making sweeping changes. Your easiest lever to pull is to simply delete stale tenant indexes as all components will fallback to bucket listing.
findings.message: Prose describes data deletion without executable restore/blast-radius preconditions. Missing condition(s): service_available_at_least.
findings.recommendation: Require a targeted scope, backup/restore validation, and capacity guard before destructive data operations.

findings.hoare_triple: { duplicate_risk is false and replay guards preserve it } action_effect: unsafe replay may set duplicate_risk=true { duplicate_risk remains false }
findings.message: queue tenant-index-fallback-scan has replayed messages without modeled deduplication
findings.recommendation: Stop replay or add deduplication/idempotency guards before messages can be processed twice.

findings.hoare_triple: { replay has dedupe_key, idempotent=true, positive dedupe window, or count=0 } action_effect: replay_messages adds messages { replay does not introduce duplicate-processing risk }
findings.message: queue tenant-index-fallback-scan replay count=18000 lacks dedupe_key, idempotency proof, or dedupe window
findings.recommendation: Add a dedupe_key, prove the handler idempotent, or model a positive dedupe_window_minutes before replay.

findings.hoare_triple: { duplicate_risk is false and replay guards preserve it } action_effect: unsafe replay may set duplicate_risk=true { duplicate_risk remains false }
findings.message: queue tenant-index-fallback-scan has replayed messages without modeled deduplication
findings.recommendation: Stop replay or add deduplication/idempotency guards before messages can be processed twice.

findings.hoare_triple: { depth=0 or target consumers>0 } action_effect: rebalance_consumers sets consumer count { backlog is not assigned zero consumers }
findings.message: queue tenant-index-fallback-scan has depth=36000 before rebalance to 0 consumers
findings.recommendation: Rebalance to a positive consumer count while backlog exists, or drain the queue first.

findings.hoare_triple: { depth=0 or consumer_group_stable=true } action_effect: rebalance/replay may leave backlog { backlog is processed only by a stable consumer group }
findings.message: queue tenant-index-fallback-scan has depth=36000 while consumer group rebalance is not stable
findings.recommendation: Wait for consumer-group stability before leaving replay/backlog processing exposed.

findings.hoare_triple: { depth=0 or consumers>0 } action_effect: replay/rebalance may change depth or consumers { backlog has at least one consumer }
findings.message: queue tenant-index-fallback-scan has depth=36000 with no active consumers
findings.recommendation: Keep at least one active consumer or drain backlog before rebalancing to zero consumers.

findings.excerpt: > If you can isolate the impacted tenants, attempt to take targeted action instead of making sweeping changes. Your easiest lever to pull is to simply delete stale tenant indexes as all components will fallback to bucket listing.
findings.message: Prose describes destructive removal/forgetting without executable blast-radius checks. Missing condition(s): service_available_at_least.
findings.recommendation: Model the removal as an action guarded by targeted scope, capacity, and rollback/restore preconditions.

findings.excerpt: machine-checkable guardrails for deduplicated fallback replay, consumer-group
findings.message: Prose mentions backfill/replay work without executable queue/backlog capacity guards. Missing condition(s): queue_depth_at_most, queue_has_consumers, queue_replay_deduplicated.
findings.recommendation: Add queue_depth_at_most, queue_has_consumers, and deduplicated replay preconditions, or document an explicit limitation.

findings.message: Suppressed destructive-delete-needs-targeting at line 19 with owner=grafana-tempo-public-fixture, expires=2099-12-31, reason=public excerpt is retained as audit evidence while the fixture models this as an explicit limitation, not a verified blast-radius proof, link=limitation:ring-forget-targeting.
findings.recommendation: Review suppression owner, expiry, reason, and invariant/waiver/limitation link during runbook review.
